They competed in the [[2025 Super League season]] and the [[2025 Challenge Cup]]. This was Salford's last season before the club was wound up by the [[High Court of Justice]] and entered [[liquidation]] over unpaid debts on 3 December 2025. ==Financial difficulties== ===Club takeover=== Salford's 2025 season was marred by financial difficulties relating to a lack of revenue from [[Salford City Council]] following their takeover of the [[Salford Community Stadium]]. In November 2024, Salford CEO Paul King asked the owners of the other eleven Super League clubs to approve a £500,000 advancement of central funding, covering staff and player wages as well as club revenues, in order to prevent the club's [[insolvency]] by the end of the year.{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Salford Red Devils financial update - player sales and Super League concerns explained |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-financial-update-30428451 |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Yorkshire Live |date=22 November 2024}}{{cite news |last1=Mulligan |first1=Alfie |title=Salford Red Devils reassure fans on financial position |url=https://salford-now-1.azurewebsites.net/2024/11/22/salford-red-devils-issue-club-statement-on-financial-position/ |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=22 November 2024}} The funding advancement was agreed by both the clubs and the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]]'s marketing branch RL Commercial later in the month.{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=RL Commercial agree to offer of advance of central distribution for Salford Red Devils as Super League club aims for Grade A in the future |url=https://www.totalrl.com/rl-commercial-agree-to-offer-of-advance-of-central-distribution-for-salford-red-devils-as-super-league-club-aims-for-grade-a-in-the-future/ |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Total Rugby League |date=25 November 2024}} As a consequence of the funding advancement, the RFL appointed [[financial regulator]]s, including former [[Wakefield Trinity]] owner Michael Carter, to oversee Salford's finances. In December 2024, a sustainability cap was imposed on Salford, requiring around £700,000, or up to 40% of the club's expenses, to be cut, necessitating plans being prepared for the sale of players.{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Salford Red Devils instructed to make seismic salary cap cuts after financial issues |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-instructed-make-30574633 |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Yorkshire Live |date=12 December 2024}} On 7 February 2025, a week prior to the commencement of the [[2025 Super League season]], Salford announced that subject to final approval by the RFL, the club had been taken over by Dario Berta, the Swiss CEO of [[investment bank]] Matanel, with the sale claimed to clear over £1.5 million in debts owed to Salford City Council. Club CEO Paul King was replaced by former Salford and [[Exeter City F.C.]] chief commercial officer Chris Irwin.{{cite news |last1=Carey |first1=Declan |title=Salford Red Devils announce new owners in major club takeover |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/greater-manchester-news/salford-red-devils-announce-new-30956846 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=7 February 2025}} ===Nonpayment of players=== {{multiple image | |total_width= 400 | image1=Kallum Watkins 2019.jpg | image2=Marc Sneyd.jpg | image3=Bradley Singleton.jpg | image4=Chris Atkin Hull KR.jpg | footer = Club captain [[Kallum Watkins]], as well as {{rlp|sh}} [[Marc Sneyd]], {{rlp|pr}} [[Brad Singleton]] and [[Chris Atkin]] all left the club between March and May 2025 }} In their Round 1 Super League fixture against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on 14 February, as a result of sustainability cap restrictions and delays in the final approval of the club's takeover, Salford controversially fielded a squad of only 16 players, with only three senior squad members - [[Joe Bullock (rugby league)|Joe Bullock]], [[Tiaki Chan]] and [[Ben Hellewell]] - present as substitutes, the remainder filled with Salford's reserves. The match, which aired on [[BBC Two]] as the BBC's first live broadcast pick of the 2025 season, ended 82-0, the highest Super League winning margin since Salford were beaten 92-12 by the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2000 Super League season|2000 season]].{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Super League left red-faced as St Helens break record in demolition of Salford Red Devils youngsters |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-left-red-faced-as-st-helens-break-record-in-demolition-of-salford-red-devils-youngsters |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=15 February 2025}}{{cite news |title=Sailor stars as 15-try St Helens thrash troubled Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cg5y9n6z024o |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=15 February 2025}} A compliance investigation into Salford's squad selection was launched by the RFL on the Monday following the game.{{cite news |title=RFL launches compliance investigation into Salford Red Devils squad selection for Super League game at St Helens |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/13311435/rfl-launches-compliance-investigation-into-salford-red-devils-squad-selection-for-super-league-game-at-st-helens |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=17 February 2025}} Following Salford's Round 3 fixture against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 27 February, it was revealed that Salford's players had not been paid on time throughout February,{{cite news |last1=Cook |first1=Sam |title=Salford Red Devils boss confirms players weren't paid and gives 'worst performance' verdict |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/salford-red-devils-boss-confirms-players-werent-paid-and-gives-worst-performance-verdict-102016/ |access-date=9 August 2025 |work=Serious About Rugby League |date=28 February 2025}} causing the players to announce intentions to refuse to attend training until their wages were paid,{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Salford Red Devils players take stand with training position over unpaid wages |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-player-wages-1006278 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=1 March 2025}} with some additionally threatening to refuse to play their Round 4 fixture against the [[Castleford Tigers]] on 7 March before later coming to an agreement with the club.{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Salford Red Devils try to convince players to play as some change mind amid talk of exits |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-try-convince-1013644 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=5 March 2025}} After it had been lifted following the approval of Dario Berta's takeover, Salford's £1.2 million sustainability cap was reimposed due to the nonpayment of wages,{{cite news |last1=Staniforth |first1=Mark |title=Salford hit with fresh salary cap sanction over non-payment of wages |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/salford-red-devils-rugby-league-b2709458.html |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=The Independent |date=5 March 2025 |location=London}} and as a result, players [[Marc Sneyd]] and [[Brad Singleton]] left the club for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the Castleford Tigers respectively,{{cite news |title=Warrington sign Salford half-back Sneyd |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cwygnke52wxo |access-date=9 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=13 March 2025}}{{cite news |title=Tigers sign Singleton from struggling Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cjd3gen3xzmo |access-date=9 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=19 March 2025}} with [[Tim Lafai]] additionally being released to return home to his family in Australia.{{cite news |last1=Mulligan |first1=Alfie |title="You're going to be hugely missed" – Tim Lafai confirms his departure from Salford Red Devils |url=https://salfordnow.co.uk/2025/03/27/salford-red-devils-star-tim-lafai-granted-his-release/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=27 March 2025}} The club's players were not paid on time again in March amid an injury crisis that took the first team squad selection to 15 players.{{cite news |last1=Mulligan |first1=Alfie |title=Salford Red Devils fail to pay staff and players on time for second time this season |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/03/28/salford-red-devils-fail-to-pay-staff-and-players-on-time-for-second-month-running/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=28 March 2025}} Club captain [[Kallum Watkins]] and [[Chris Atkin]], who left the club for [[Leeds Rhinos]] and Castleford Tigers respectively later that month,{{cite news |title=Leeds re-sign former captain Watkins from Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cwy6nkkw85jo |access-date=9 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=8 April 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Mulligan |first1=Alfie |title=Salford Red Devils Chris Atkin joins Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/04/30/salford-red-devils-chris-atkin-joins-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=9 August 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=30 April 2025}} were reported to have refused to play their Round 6 fixture against [[Wigan Warriors]] as a result, each being threatened with a £2,000 fine and the withholding of their player registration by the RFL.{{cite news |title=Brierley opens up on Salford players' mental turmoil |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cq6744jn4mqo |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=31 March 2025}} CEO Chris Irwin admitted in April that the club's debts to Salford City Council were not cleared by the sale to Dario Berta in February and also stated that an ownership consortium running the club, consisting of Saia Kalahi and Kurt Graver, "are not rugby league people", instead suggesting that they are more interested in developing the land surrounding the Salford Community Stadium and granting the Red Devils 20% ownership of the stadium.{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=Salford Red Devils chief executive Chris Irwin gives latest on ailing Super League club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-chief-executive-chris-irwin-gives-latest-on-ailing-super-league-club/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Total Rugby League |date=10 April 2025}} Irwin later resigned as CEO on 6 May after less than three months in the position,{{cite news |title=Chief executive Irwin resigns at troubled Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c8x8xj9wp72o |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=6 May 2025}} with Paul King returning to the club in an "advisory position", having paid players' wages despite not being a member of Salford's staff and put up his house as [[Collateral (finance)|collateral]] against the club's debts.{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Danny |title=Ex-Salford Red Devils CEO steps back in to try and save the club from collapse |url=https://themanc.com/sport/ex-salford-red-devils-ceo-paul-king-returns-paying-player-wages-saving-the-club/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=The Manc |date=12 May 2025}} ===Winding-up petition=== {{multiple image | |total_width= 400 | image1=Ryan Brierley.jpg | image2=Chris Hankinson Wigan.jpg | image3=Chris Hill.jpg | image4=Jack Ormondroyd Fev.jpg | footer = Replacement captain [[Ryan Brierley]] and top goal scorer [[Chris Hankinson]], as well as {{rlp|pr}}s [[Chris Hill (rugby league)|Chris Hill]] and [[Jack Ormondroyd]], among others, all left the club in August 2025 after it was revealed their [[pension]]s were not being paid }} On 14 May 2025, [[His Majesty's Revenue and Customs]] (HMRC) filed a [[winding-up]] petition against Salford City Reds (2013) Limited, the operating company for Salford Red Devils, after it was revealed the club owed HMRC £500,000 in debts.{{cite news |last1=Ward-Brennan |first1=Maria |title=Salford Red Devils hit with winding-up by tax authorities |url=https://www.cityam.com/salford-red-devils-hit-with-winding-up-by-tax-authorities/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=City AM |date=14 May 2025}} A day later, a bid by Salford to buy the Salford Community Stadium outright through Jacobson Management fell through when Salford City Council pulled out of negotiations with the company.{{cite news |title=Council rejects Salford's attempts to buy stadium |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czj43mpll2xo |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=15 May 2025}} For a third time, Salford's players were not paid on time throughout May, however players received their pay only a day late from an outside company and not the club's accounts.{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Salford Red Devils players paid with the source revealed |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-players-paid-1179948 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=30 May 2025}} [[Sam Stone (rugby league)|Sam Stone]] departed the club on loan to Warrington Wolves for the rest of the season on 17 June after having been accused by coach [[Paul Rowley]] of "downing tools" by making himself unavailable for squad selection in the prior Round 14 fixture against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cite news |title=Stone moves to Warrington on loan from Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c36xn3533kro |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=17 June 2025}} After having announced that a [[bridging loan]] was to arrive to help pay both HMRC debts and players and staff wages, on 25 June, the winding-up petition against Salford City Reds (2013) Limited was adjourned until 3 September.{{cite news |title=Winding-up petition against Salford adjourned |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39zvjwdxg1o |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=25 June 2025}} Between that period, however, following the threat of a players' strike in the Round 19 fixture against the Leeds Rhinos,{{cite news |title=Rowley 'unaware' of Salford player strike threat |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cql014eq6kyo |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=16 July 2025}} the situation escalated significantly after it was confirmed in August 2025 that Salford had not been paying its players' [[pension]]s,{{cite web |last1=Davidson |first1=John |title=Salford's financial problems worsen |url=https://www.patreon.com/posts/salfords-worsen-135758329 |publisher=rugbyleaguehub.com Long Reads |access-date=10 August 2025 |date=5 August 2025}} with only two months' worth of pensions paid to players over the course of 2025; with non-playing staff also not being paid through July, this was followed with the exits of club captain [[Ryan Brierley]], top goal scorer [[Chris Hankinson]], [[Chris Hill (rugby league)|Chris Hill]] and [[Jack Ormondroyd]] from the club days prior to Salford's Round 21 fixture against [[Hull F.C.]] on 10 August.{{cite news |last1=Davidson |first1=John |title=The inside story of how Salford Red Devils were brought to their knees |url=https://inews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/how-salford-red-devils-were-brought-to-their-knees-3849974 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=The i Paper |date=8 August 2025}} Salford's fulfilment of the fixture against Hull F.C. was further in doubt due to unpaid debts for a coach transport company and a laundrette responsible for washing the team's kits.{{dubious|reason=Possible hearsay, none of the bigger or local publications are reporting this.|date=August 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Salford Red Devils make four signings for Hull FC clash as 'dire' unpaid bill situation laid out |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-make-two-1317342 |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=8 August 2025}} The squad for the fixture eventually included ten players on loan, two of which came from Leeds Rhinos on a week's loan, after regulations against loans were relaxed by special dispensation of the RFL,{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Peter |title=Leeds Rhinos news: first team duo join crisis hit Salford Red Devils on emergency loan for Hull FC game |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-news-first-team-duo-join-crisis-hit-salford-red-devils-on-emergency-loan-for-hull-fc-game-5262468 |work=Yorkshire Evening Post |location=Leeds |date=8 August 2025 |access-date=9 August 2025}} as well as [[Leigh Leopards]]' [[Brad Dwyer]], whose late addition after the submission of the initial squad saw Salford docked one [[Substitution (sport)|interchange]] for the match.{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Salford Red Devils squad for Hull FC clash locked in as TEN loanees included |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-squad-for-hull-fc-clash-locked-in-as-ten-loanees-included |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=10 August 2025}} Salford ultimately lost this match 80-6, with a consolation try and conversion provided by loanees Brad Dwyer and [[Riley Lumb]] respectively, as well as fan protests early in the match, being applauded by both sets of fans at the [[MKM Stadium]].{{cite news |last1=Rostance |first1=Tom |title=14-try Hull hammer makeshift Salford side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cdd32ed84mqo |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport}} Following a near-lack of communication throughout the season since the takeover, Dario Berta's ownership consortium released a statement on 11 August declaring that "Salford Red Devils will not close", stating that the club was dealing with £3 million of inherited debt, that an external backer's "essential £1.8m investment" failed to materialise in November 2024, and that the bridging loan would be delivered to the club by the end of August.{{cite news |title=Salford owners say club 'will not close' |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c1kzwemyvy7o |access-date=13 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=12 August 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Salford owners finally break silence as defiant promises and financial pledges made |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-owners-break-silence-financial-promises-made |access-date=13 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=11 August 2025}} A supporter's group, 'The 1873', set up to demand "urgent answers" from a "silent and neglectful" ownership,{{cite news |last1=Thompson |first1=Doug |title=Salford Red Devils 'left to rot' by owners says Supporters' Trust chair |url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-left-to-rot-by-owners-says-supporters-trust-chair/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Total Rugby League |date=8 August 2025}} labelled the statement "nothing more than a wall of excuses, finger-pointing and deliberate falsehoods" in response a day later, and has planned protest action at the Round 22 home fixture against [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cite news |last1=Chapman Coombe |first1=Louis |title=Salford Red Devils supporters hit back at owners as protest vow made in scathing statement |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-supporters-hit-back-at-owners-as-protest-vow-made-in-scathing-statement |access-date=13 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=12 August 2025}} During August, [[Charlie Glover]] departed from the club for semi-professional [[rugby union]] team [[Preston Grasshoppers R.F.C.]],{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Latest Salford Red Devils exit confirmed as young gun makes rugby union switch |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/latest-salford-red-devils-exit-confirmed-as-young-gun-makes-rugby-union-switch |access-date=13 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=13 August 2025}} while [[Tom Whitehead (rugby league)|Tom Whitehead]] was recalled from his rolling monthly loan by Warrington and signed on loan to [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] for the remainder of the club's [[2025 RFL Championship]] campaign.{{cite news |last1=Riley |first1=George |title=Salford Red Devils' latest blow confirmed as Warrington Wolves send loanee to Championship |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-latest-blow-confirmed-as-warrington-wolves-send-loanee-to-championship |access-date=14 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=14 August 2025}} ===Match cancellation=== Salford's [[chief operating officer]] Claire Bradbury, who had been appointed to the role in October 2024, resigned from the position and the club on 14 August; in a post on her [[LinkedIn]] profile, she accused the club's ownership{{vague|reason=Under whose ownership was the comment made?|date=August 2025}} of suggesting she "sleep with an individual at the RFL to 'smooth things over'".{{cite news |title=Salford Red Devils: Claire Bradbury resigns as chief operating officer and alleges she was told to 'sleep with individual' at RFL |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13411734/salford-red-devils-claire-bradbury-resigns-as-chief-operating-officer-and-alleges-she-was-told-to-sleep-with-individual-at-rfl |access-date=14 August 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=14 August 2025}} Players and staff were not paid again on time during July, and doubts were made for the fulfilment of the Round 22 Wakefield Trinity fixture as other clubs recalled their loaned players and were not willing to make further loans to Salford.{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Update on Salford's clash with Wakefield Trinity as Red Devils frantically source players |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/update-on-salfords-clash-with-wakefield-trinity-as-red-devils-frantically-source-players |access-date=14 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=13 August 2025}} Ultimately, the fixture was cancelled on 15 August following discussion with the RFL on the grounds of "significant welfare concerns",{{cite news |title=Salford v Wakefield off over welfare concerns |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cjeyz09313do |access-date=15 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=15 August 2025}} with Wakefield awarded a 48-0 [[Forfeit (sport)|forfeit]] victory on 20 August by the RFL.{{cite news |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity handed 48-0 victory after Salford Red Devils fixture cancellation |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13413978/super-league-wakefield-trinity-handed-48-0-victory-after-salford-red-devils-fixture-cancellation |access-date=20 August 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=20 August 2025}} The Round 23 fixture against the Leigh Leopards went ahead on 22 August following the loans of [[Leon Ruan]] and [[Louix Gorman]] from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Jack Darbyshire]] from Leigh Leopards and [[Neil Tchamambe]] from Wakefield Trinity to the club,{{cite news |title=Salford Red Devils: Super League crisis club confirm Leigh Leopards clash to go ahead on Friday |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/13414303/salford-red-devils-super-league-crisis-club-confirm-leigh-leopards-clash-to-go-ahead-on-friday |access-date=20 August 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=19 August 2025}} resulting in a 6-38 loss to Leigh. Despite scoring a shock 25-12 win away at Warrington Wolves in Round 24 on 29 August, on 1 September, Salford's assistant coach [[Kurt Haggerty]], who was to be appointed as head coach to replace Paul Rowley from 2026, left the club by mutual consent.{{cite news |title=Salford boss-in-waiting Haggerty leaves club |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cj6y6ekwlk4o |access-date=2 September 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=1 September 2025}} On 3 September, the winding-up petition against the club was adjourned for a second time to 29 October, falling over a month after the conclusion of Salford's Super League season.{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Salford winding-up petition adjourned again |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2lx11w47kro |access-date=3 September 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=3 September 2025}}{{cite news |title=Salford Red Devils handed further adjournment in HMRC hearing as financial crisis continues |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/13424064/salford-red-devils-handed-further-adjournment-in-hmrc-hearing-as-financial-crisis-continues |access-date=3 September 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=3 September 2025}} ===Post-season=== Salford ended their 2025 season in 12th place following a defeat to Wakefield Trinity, a match which saw numerous protests by both sets of supporters as well as two [[pitch invasion]]s late in the match by Salford fans.{{cite news |last1=Hayton |first1=Edward |title=Tensions spill over as troubled Salford Red Devils end Super League campaign in defeat |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/09/20/tensions-spill-over-as-troubled-salford-red-devils-end-super-league-campaign-in-defeat/ |access-date=24 September 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=20 September 2025}} On 22 September, Salford were hit with a points deduction and a £5,000 fine by the RFL, half of which was suspended until 2029, for breaching "a number of operational rules" for their sixteen-player team selection in the Round 1 fixture against St Helens. As a result, Salford finished the season with a total of four league points instead of six.{{cite news |title=Salford given points deduction for team selection |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2ry9zy78ro |access-date=24 September 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=22 September 2025}} When applications for [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|IMG Grading]] for inclusion in the 14-team [[2026 Super League season]] were announced by the RFL on 16 September, Salford were not among the nine clubs that applied for Super League status.{{cite news |last1=Hayton |first1=Edward |title=A "Deep disappointment" – supporters react as Salford Red Devils back out of Super League application |url=https://salfordnow.co.uk/2025/09/16/a-deep-disappointment-supporters-react-as-salford-red-devils-back-out-of-super-league-application/ |access-date=17 September 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=16 September 2025}} Salford's relegation was confirmed by the promotion of [[Bradford Bulls]] to the Super League on 16 October, bringing an end to Salford's 17-year stay in the Super League.{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Bradford replace Salford in Super League for 2026 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c874040rjlno |access-date=16 October 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=16 October 2025}} ===Liquidation=== On 3 December 2025, after two more hearings were consecutively adjourned to allow opportunities for the club to pay back it's debts,{{cite news |last1=Carey |first1=Declan |title=Future of Salford Red Devils hangs in balance after third adjournment in HMRC court case |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/greater-manchester-news/future-salford-red-devils-hangs-32769739 |access-date=3 December 2025 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=29 October 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Hayton |first1=Edward |title=Salford Red Devils winding-up petition adjourned for fourth time |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/11/12/salford-red-devils-winding-up-petition-adjourned-for-fourth-time/ |access-date=3 December 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=12 November 2025}} Salford City Reds (2013) Limited was wound up by the High Court during a one-minute hearing over the unpaid debt to HMRC.{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Salford Red Devils wound up by High Court over debts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm2wvenzkrmo |access-date=3 December 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=3 December 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Cuffing |first1=Ethan |title=Salford Red Devils liquidated in one-minute court hearing |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/12/03/salford-red-devils-liquidated-in-court-hearing/ |access-date=3 December 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=3 December 2025}} The club's playing licence was revoked by the RFL following the ruling, meaning the club were not able to play in the [[2026 RFL Championship]],{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=RFL outline Salford Red Devils timeline as membership terminated following liquidation |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rfl-outline-salford-red-devils-timeline-as-membership-terminated-following-liquidation |access-date=3 December 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=3 December 2025}} however a [[phoenix club (sports)|phoenix club]] named [[Salford RLFC]], a consortium led by former player [[Mason Caton-Brown]], was approved to take Salford Red Devils' place in the Championship on 22 December.{{cite news |title=New Salford team granted Championship status |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/clyd8n213pmo |access-date=22 December 2025 |work=BBC table|showteam=SAL}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2025 Challenge Cup}} On 25 June 2024, the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] announced a change to the Challenge Cup format, totalling 7 rounds compared to the previous 9, with Super League teams entering to play away from home at round 3.{{Cite web |date=25 June 2024 |title=New format for Betfred Challenge Cup in 2025 |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62890/new-format-for-betfred-challenge-cup-in-2025 |access-date=8 March 2025 |website=[[Rugby Football League]]}} Salford were first drawn to play the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] away in Round 3, following Midlands' 46-0 victory over [[Siddal A.R.L.F.C.]] on 26 January.{{cite news |last1=Cuffling |first1=Ethan |title=Salford Red Devils set to face Midlands Hurricanes in Challenge Cup Third Round |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/01/28/salford-red-devils-to-face-midlands-hurricanes-in-challenge-cup-third-round/ |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=28 January 2025}} Following their victory against Midlands, Salford were drawn on 15 February to play the [[Bradford Bulls]] at home on 14 March,{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Challenge Cup Fourth Round draw as 11 Super League sides discover opponents |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-fourth-round-draw-as-11-super-league-sides-discover-opponents |access-date=8 March 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=15 February 2025}} and after a 26-16 victory over Bradford, Salford entered the quarter-finals and were drawn to play the [[Catalans Dragons]] at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]].{{cite news |last1=Cuffing |first1=Ethan |title=Salford Red Devils to face Catalans Dragons in the Challenge Cup quarter-finals |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2025/03/18/salford-red-devils-to-face-catalans-dragons-in-the-challenge-cup-quarter-finals/ |access-date=10 August 2025 |work=Salford Now |date=18 March 2025}}
